response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo is a versatile full-stack engineer with genuine breadth across web, mobile, and systems work, but critically fails the minimum qualifications for this role. The hard requirement of 8 years of C++ experience is not met — her C++ exposure amounts to roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, and C++ does not appear in her listed skills. The role also demands 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or distributed systems experience and meaningful cloud compute leadership; while her Dunnhumby/Sociomantic work shows real high-throughput systems chops (~2 years, Node.js/DLang) and she has touched AWS CDK at Optilyz, her overall trajectory is firmly in web and full-stack engineering rather than cloud compute platform development.
strengths: Real high-throughput distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O
Polyglot adaptability across TypeScript, DLang, Node.js, Java, C#, and Swift, showing capacity to operate across diverse technical environments
Demonstrated community leadership through open-source projects, conference talks, and multi-day technical workshops
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at NI plus a thesis) versus the non-negotiable 8-year minimum, which alone disqualifies the candidacy
No evidence of leading cloud compute platform teams or owning large-scale cloud infrastructure at production depth and scope
Career trajectory is squarely web/full-stack; there is no progression toward systems-level or cloud infrastructure engineering
